description: You are a computational physicist at a national laboratory. The lightweight numerical methods library `numlib` at /home/ga/workspace/numlib/ has 5 subtle mathematical/algorithmic bugs across its core modules: numerical integration, ODE solving, matrix decomposition, spline interpolation, and root finding. Identify and fix these bugs so that the library's test suite passes. You must fix the scratch implementations within the library rather than bypassing the algorithms using external libraries like `scipy` or `numpy`.
